items.question: Is UNILIFT certified for use in the United States and Canada?
items.answer:
Yes. North American UNILIFT models are certified to UL 1323 and CSA Z271 for powered suspended scaffold and suspended access applications.

Certification applies to the specified hoist model and configuration. Always verify the certification markings, voltage, capacity, and serial number shown on the equipment nameplate.

items.question: Is UNILIFT certified for lifting people?
items.answer:
Yes. UNILIFT is designed and certified for personnel lifting on powered suspended scaffolds, swing stages, and suspended access platforms.

“Man-riding hoist” is commonly used internationally. In the United States, “personnel-lifting traction hoist” or “powered suspended scaffold hoist” is more conventional.

The complete platform, rigging system, fall protection, and installation must also comply with applicable jobsite requirements.

items.question: Does a UL-certified hoist make my suspended scaffold OSHA compliant?
items.answer:
No single component makes the complete scaffold OSHA compliant.

UNILIFT provides a certified hoist for integration into a properly designed suspended scaffold system. The complete installation must comply with OSHA 29 CFR 1926 Subpart L, applicable state and local requirements, and relevant standards such as ANSI/ASSP A10.8.

The employer, qualified person, and competent person remain responsible for system design, rigging, inspection, training, fall protection, and safe operation.

items.question: Which UNILIFT model do I need: single-phase or three-phase?
items.answer:
UNILIFT is available in three North American configurations:

1,500 lb capacity, 208 V single-phase
1,500 lb capacity, 208 V three-phase
2,200 lb capacity, 208 V three-phase

The single-phase model is suitable for jobsites where three-phase power is unavailable. Three-phase models offer lower current draw and include the maximum-capacity 2,200 lb configuration.

Model selection should consider platform capacity, number of hoists, available power, working height, and cable length.

items.question: What is the maximum lifting height?
items.answer:
UNILIFT has no fixed drum-capacity limit.

The wire rope passes continuously through the traction mechanism instead of winding onto a drum. Practical lifting height is therefore determined by the deployed wire rope, electrical cable, rigging arrangement, and jobsite requirements.

items.question: How large and heavy is the UNILIFT hoist?
items.answer:
The North American UNILIFT weighs approximately 159 lb.

Its nominal dimensions include:

Height: 30.2 inches
Width: 11.8 inches
Wire rope: 3/8 inch
Lifting speed: approximately 35 feet per minute

Refer to the technical drawing before designing mounting brackets, stirrups, or platform interfaces.

items.question: Can UNILIFT replace a Tirak, Sky Climber, Spider, Power Climber, or Bisomac hoist?
items.answer:
UNILIFT operates in the same powered suspended scaffold hoist category as Tirak, Sky Climber, Spider, Power Climber, and Bisomac equipment.

A direct replacement cannot be assumed. Manufacturers may use different mounting interfaces, wire-rope constructions, electrical connectors, control systems, capacities, and safety devices.

FAS-Tech can review your existing scaffold motor or traction hoist configuration and identify the required UNILIFT model, mounting arrangement, and electrical connection.

items.question: What happens if the suspension rope breaks or the hoist overspeeds?
items.answer:
UNILIFT includes an independent OSLA overspeed safety lock.

If the primary suspension rope fails or the platform exceeds the permitted descent speed, the OSLA automatically grips the independent safety wire rope and stops the platform.

The safety lock is factory-installed and electrically interlocked with the hoist.

items.question: What happens if power fails during operation?
items.answer:
The electromagnetic brake engages automatically and holds the load.

A trained operator can use the manual brake-release system to perform a controlled emergency descent. The procedure must follow the operating manual and should only be performed by personnel trained on the equipment.

items.question: Can I use any 3/8-inch wire rope?
items.answer:
No. Matching the diameter alone is insufficient.

UNILIFT must only operate with FAS-Tech-authorized 3/8-inch wire rope of the specified construction, strength, lay, and condition. Using an incompatible rope can reduce traction, damage the hoist, or prevent the safety system from operating correctly.

Do not reuse rope from a Tirak, Sky Climber, Spider, Power Climber, or other scaffold hoist unless FAS-Tech has confirmed compatibility.

items.question: What are the lead times and warranty?
items.answer: Both are confirmed with your quote, based on configuration and quantity. Send us your timeline and we'll come back with dates.

items.question: Can our technicians service UNILIFT themselves?
items.answer:
Qualified technicians can perform routine inspections and maintenance by following the UNILIFT service manual.

The hoist uses maintenance procedures familiar to suspended scaffold technicians and does not require proprietary service tools for standard work. An integrated hour meter supports accurate maintenance records, with routine service intervals and a 500-operating-hour overhaul cycle.

Major repairs and overhauls must be completed by trained and authorized personnel.

items.question: Do you have U.S. distributors and service partners?
items.answer:
FAS-Tech currently supports U.S. customers directly while developing its distributor and service network.

Contact FAS-Tech to confirm available sales, training, spare-parts, and technical-service coverage for your state. Distribution and service territories remain open for qualified suspended-access companies.

Third-party company and product names are trademarks of their respective owners. They are referenced only to identify comparable equipment and compatibility considerations.
